_comment: Regulatory frameworks, design goals/constraints and occupancy factors. All values editable; every entry cites references.json. The UI lets the user pick a framework, then shows and allows overriding the design goal P.

NCRP.name: NCRP design goals (weekly air kerma)
NCRP.description: Shielding design goals P expressed as air kerma at the reference point (0.3 m beyond the barrier), per NCRP Report 147 Section 1.4 and NCRP Report 151. Controlled areas: occupationally exposed workers; uncontrolled: public and non-radiation workers.

controlled.note: Fraction of the 50 mSv/y occupational limit; consistent with cumulative-dose guidance (10 mSv x age).

IAEA_NRRC.name: IAEA GSR Part 3 / Saudi NRRC-R-01 (annual dose constraints)
IAEA_NRRC.description: Annual dose limits from IAEA GSR Part 3 Schedule III as adopted in Saudi Arabia by NRRC-R-01 (2022) Section 10. Design uses a dose CONSTRAINT below the limit (NRRC-R-01 clause 20 requires practice-specific constraints but sets no generic number, so the recommended international values below are defaults the user may edit).

dose_limits.occupational_averaging: averaged over 5 consecutive years (100 mSv in 5 y), max 50 mSv in any single year

design_constraints_default.controlled_note: 3/10 of the occupational limit - common international practice for designing controlled areas (IAEA SRS 47 Section 2; also used in pre-2004 IAEA BSS-115 methodology).
design_constraints_default.uncontrolled_note: 0.3 mSv/y public dose constraint per source/practice - value used by European codes (RPII 09/01) and consistent with IAEA guidance that a single source should not consume the whole 1 mSv/y public limit.

linac_dose_rate_checks._comment: Additional NCRP 151-style checks for megavoltage vaults, applied on top of the weekly/annual goal.
linac_dose_rate_checks.description: Time-averaged dose in any one hour (TADR-based check) for uncontrolled areas.

occupancy_factors._comment: NCRP 147 Table 4.1 'Suggested occupancy factors (for use as a guide in planning shielding where other occupancy data are not available)'. Verified against the provided PDF, document page 31 (PDF page 41).

table.areas: Administrative or clerical offices; laboratories, pharmacies and other work areas fully occupied by an individual; receptionist areas, attended waiting rooms, children's indoor play areas, adjacent x-ray rooms, film reading areas, nurse's stations, x-ray control rooms
Rooms used for patient examinations and treatments
Corridors, patient rooms, employee lounges, staff rest rooms
Public toilets, unattended vending areas, storage rooms, outdoor areas with seating, unattended waiting rooms, patient holding areas
Outdoor areas with only transient pedestrian or vehicular traffic, unattended parking lots, vehicular drop off areas (unattended), attics, stairways, unattended elevators, janitor's closets

notes: T is the fraction of beam-on time that the SINGLE most-exposed person spends in the area, not the fraction of time the area is occupied by anyone.
Controlled areas (x-ray rooms, control booths) are designed with T = 1.
When using a low T for an area immediately adjacent to the x-ray room, also check more distant fully-occupied areas - they may govern the design despite the larger distance.
